world.title: The next oil shock is coming, and it won’t come from Hormuz
world.description: A second major shock to the global economy is emerging as the ongoing disruption in the Strait of Hormuz is now compounded by the risk of Houthi attacks on Red Sea shipping. Iran is reportedly pushing the Houthis to prepare for action, which could disrupt the Bab el-Mandeb strait—another critical trade route—creating simultaneous pressure on two key maritime chokepoints.

world.title: Trump willing to end Iran war without reopening Strait of Hormuz, according to reports
world.description: US and Iranian officials also issued contradictory statements as to whether negotiations are taking place to end the war, as strikes continue across the region. | ITV National News

world.title: Pope Leo XIV: The Most Difficult Circumstances Can Be Transformed by the Power of Love
world.description: In an audience with the Illinois Municipal League, the pontiff said that Gospel values can be applied to city government to the benefit of the most vulnerable and for the common good.

world.title: Joe Kent blames Israel, ‘Truman Show’ around Donald Trump for US-Iran war: ‘Like Iraq’
world.description: According to the former official, Donald Trump was sceptical about the Iran war, but the “echo chamber with the Truman show” around him “did a great job”. | World News

world.title: Kenya Risks Defaulting Debts Worth Ksh3.32 Trillion, Including Bonds - Controller of Budget
world.description: Hello and welcome to the Money News Roundup Newsletter, where we cover the warning by the Controller of Budget on the risks of Kenya defaulting on its loan obligations. We also cover peanut butter brands that have been banned for sale by the Ministry of Health.

world.title: Request for Applications: The Bekker Nawa Programme (Poland)
world.description: Deadline: 29-May-2026 The Bekker Nawa Programme funds Polish doctoral students and scientists for research stays at leading international academic centres. The programme promotes scientific mobility, international collaboration, and career development across all disciplines, with a total budget of 20,000,000 PLN. Grant Overview:This programme supports researchers in completing part of their doctoral education or research abroad,

world.title: Germany news: Merz and Syrian president talk refugee returns
world.description: As Syrian President Ahmad al-Sharaa visits Berlin, Chancellor Friedrich Merz says about 80% of Syrians living in Germany are expected to return home. Meanwhile, fears are mounting about inflation. DW has the latest.

world.title: RFPs: Healthcare and Civilian Safety Support in Kharkiv (Ukraine)
world.description: Deadline: 05-May-2026 UNOPS is offering grants to strengthen healthcare systems and civilian protection in Kharkiv oblast, Ukraine. Key interventions include hospital basement repairs, medical equipment delivery, and face-to-face Explosive Ordnance Risk Education (EORE) for vulnerable civilians, with funding up to US$235,000 per project. business.title: ‘We are what keeps this country rolling’: Hauliers call for solutions to fuel crisis
business.description: Ger Hyland, Irish Road Haulage Association president, has said temporary measures taken to ease fuel prices have already been 'swallowed up' by subsequent hikes in the price of oil.

business.title: Jaecoo J8 facelift in China – badged Chery Tiggo 9, gains massive touchscreen, 261 PS 2.0T 4
business.description: Launched in Malaysia just last year, the Jaecoo J8 is set to get a massive facelift ahead of the Auto China show in Beijing next month. Except it won’t be badged as a Jaecoo – …

business.title: LIST: NAIA terminal reassignments for foreign airlines
business.description: New NAIA Infra Corp. (NNIC), the private operator of the Ninoy Aquino International Airport (NAIA), has released a list of terminal reassignments for foreign airlines at the Manila gateway effective March 29 and April 1, 2026—in time for the Holy Week travel.

business.title: Nigeria’s Eurobonds extend bearish run in March as investors reprice risk

business.title: CancerVax Reports Successful Initial Mouse Study with Breakthrough Results
business.description: First biodistribution study of the Company’s novel targeted lipid nanoparticle achieved systemic circulation with the potential to target many types of cancer

business.title: NPA sets new fuel price floors for April 1-15
business.description: The National Petroleum Authority (NPA) has announced new minimum price floors for petroleum products for the April 1–15, 2026 pricing window, a move expected to push fuel prices higher at the pumps.

science.title: Starlink satellite experiences on-orbit anomaly, SpaceX confirms
science.description: US aerospace company SpaceX confirmed on Monday that a Starlink satellite experienced an on-orbit anomaly on Sunday, resulting in a loss of communications and the generation of debris.

science.title: Eight more satellites added to IRIDE space programme
science.description: Eight satellites have been added to Italy’s IRIDE Earth observation programme, following launch on board a Falcon-9 rocket. The successful launch brings the total number of satellites in orbit for the Italian programme to 24.

science.title: Concentric rocky rings adorned with ancient artwork wear a magma 'hat' in the Sahara - Earth from space
science.description: A 2025 astronaut photo shows a massif made of concentric mountain ridges in the Libyan desert. The rocky walls contain ancient artworks and are occasionally used to contain herds of grazing cattle.

science.title: Not From Deep Space: New Study Reveals Where Earth Was Truly Born
science.description: A new isotope-based study finds Earth formed entirely from inner Solar System material, contradicting earlier theories of outer Solar System contribution.

science.title: What is the Artemis II mission and why is NASA going back to the moon?
science.description: Humans are set to return for the first time in 53 years. The new era of lunar travel isn’t about reaching space, but staying there.

science.title: Identifying Limits Of Protein Evolution
science.description: The number of known proteins is infinitely small in comparison to the universe of possible proteins which could in theory be realized. Yet these known

science.title: Black Hole Runs Out of Gas, Rapidly Dims its Galaxy
science.description: It's not often that astronomers can observe huge changes in a galaxy's brightness over the course of a few years. Most galaxies change in brightness (and other characteristics) over millions or billions of years. So, when images of the 10-billion-light-year distant galaxy J0218-0036 showed that it dimmed down by a twentieth of its previous brightness in just 20 years, observers were surprised. science.title: The Universe has changed by the time you finish this sentence
science.description: In a 13.8 billion year old Universe, a few seconds hardly seems like it matters. But these minuscule changes sure do add up over time.

ai.title: How AI Companion Platforms Are Reshaping Digital Payments, Trust, and User Experience
ai.description: Explore how AI companion platforms are transforming digital payments, trust, and user experience through personalization, subscription models, and fintech-driven innovation.

ai.title: AI-Doctored Video Scandal Hits Maharashtra: Top Minister Implicated
ai.description: A case has been registered against the brother of a transperson for allegedly circulating an AI-doctored video featuring Maharashtra Minister Narhari Zirwal. The video purportedly shows the minister in a compromising position. The accused allegedly used the video for blackmail, demanding money or a job. Investigations are ongoing.

ai.title: As developers claim Apple is pushing back on Vibecoding apps, iPhone maker says: It has always been very clear that no app ...
ai.description: Tech News News: Apple is reportedly facing questions on how it is handling new category of AI-powered app development tools, as developers claim restrictions on such .

ai.title: The US now has more spas than shops - and more data centres than offices
ai.description: A major shift is underway in US real estate, as booming AI demand pushes data centre construction past office spending, signalling a new era for digital infrastructure investment.

ai.title: Marc Andreessen says AI is silver bullet excuse for layoffs, real reason is something else
ai.description: Venture capitalist Marc Andreessen says AI is not the real reason behind the recent layoffs, it’s just an easy excuse companies are using. The real reason, he argues, is that firms over hired during COVID and are now correcting the course.

ai.title: P. R. Ramesh Joins VerSe Innovation Board as Independent Director
ai.description: Mr. P. R. Ramesh, ex-Chairman of Deloitte India, joins VerSe Innovation as an Independent Director and Chair of the Audit Committee. Bringing four decades of corporate governance experience, he will help strengthen VerSe's governance as it grows. VerSe is known for its AI-powered local language platforms, like Dailyhunt and Josh.
